pests.name: Whiteflies
pests.solution: Spray neem solution, cover crop with a living fence like maize, intercrop with maize, spray water on the crop.

diseases.name: Cassava Leaf Mosaic Virus (SLCMV)
diseases.impact: Yellow-green mosaic patterns on leaves, leaf curling and reduced leaf area, irregular leaf shape, stunting of plants, gradual weakening of plants, and reduced yield.
diseases.solution: No treatment once infected. Use only stem cuttings from healthy plants. Uproot and destroy diseased plants. Remove wild cassava, wild clove, wild rubber, and Kuppamenia from the field. Do not start a new cultivation near a diseased one; first remove all diseased plants from the old cultivation. Always remove weeds around the crop and keep the field clean. Mark diseased plant stems with paint to facilitate removal during harvest.

diseases.name: Brown Spot Disease
diseases.impact: Brown spots on leaves. Mature lower leaves are usually infected first, with upper leaves also getting infected when damage is severe. Leaves turn yellow and fall off.
diseases.solution: In home gardens, remove and burn infected leaves from the field. In commercial cultivations, when the disease is prevalent in young plants, apply Mancozeb 80% WP (32g per 16-liter tank of water) to leaves to control spread.

diseases.name: Root Rot
diseases.impact: Rotting of roots and stems of plants in patches. Leaves turn yellow, plant dries up and dies. White fungal mycelium visible at the base of the plant.
diseases.solution: Improve soil drainage. If it's a paddy field, improve drainage by digging deep canals. Uproot diseased plants along with soil and remove from the field at the early stages of the disease.
